content.problemStatement:
You are given KKK different colors of beads. A bead of the iii-th color has a weight wiw_iwi​. You have infinitely many beads of each color. All the beads of the same color have the same weight.

You have to answer qqq independent queries. For each query, you will be given SSS. You have to form a necklace consisting of nnn beads such that the total weight of the necklace is SSS. You can use any number of beads of a single color (possibly zero). Count the number of distinct possible necklaces you can make. Two necklaces will be considered the same if one can be rotated to another.


Input Format

First line contains nnn, denoting the size of the necklace.
Second line contains KKK, denoting the number of different colors.
Third line contains KKK space separated integers, where the iii-th integer wiw_iwi​ denotes the weight of a bead of color iii.
Fourth line contains qqq, denoting the number of queries.
Each of the next qqq lines will contain a single integer SSS, denoting the total weight of the necklace for this query.


Output Format
For each query, output in a new line, the number of valid necklaces mod 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: nnn will be a power of two

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For S=6S = 6S=6, three ways to form the necklace are:

1→1→1→31 \rightarrow 1 \rightarrow 1 \rightarrow 31→1→1→3
1→1→2→21 \rightarrow 1 \rightarrow 2 \rightarrow 21→1→2→2
1→2→1→21 \rightarrow 2 \rightarrow 1 \rightarrow 21→2→1→2

Please note, we do not count 2→1→1→22 \rightarrow 1 \rightarrow 1 \rightarrow 22→1→1→2 because it is just a rotation of 1→1→2→21 \rightarrow 1 \rightarrow 2 \rightarrow 21→1→2→2.
